data.acceptance:
# Acceptance criteria

The chosen path (A or B, decided in planning) determines which of the
two AC sets below applies. Both end at the same place: the umbrella's
self-audit grep #2 returns zero unsanctioned hits.

## Path A — Contract doc update

### AC-A1 — Sanctioned exceptions subsection added

`docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` § "Canonical Functions" gains
a "Sanctioned exceptions" subsection naming all 5 file paths and
explaining the registration-boundary rule.

### AC-A2 — Self-audit grep #2 updated

The grep one-liner in § "Self-Audit" filters out the 5 sanctioned
files (e.g. via additional `grep -v` lines or a single `grep -Ev`
with the file pattern) so it returns zero hits when the P0 sibling
ships.

### AC-A3 — Doc cross-link

The 5 settings files each get a brief inline comment at the import
line referencing the contract doc's "Sanctioned exceptions"
subsection — so the next person reading the code knows immediately
why this isn't a violation.

## Path B — Refactor to persist canonical form

### AC-B1 — Settings stores canonical fields

`settings.toml` agent entries persist
`{ agent_type, vendor, provider, agent_name, lease_model, version,
flavor }` in canonical form. Re-reading does not require
normalisation.

### AC-B2 — Single canonical call at write time

`normalizeAgentIdentity` is called exactly once per registration
event (write-side), in `agent-config-normalization.ts` or
`settings-agent-scan.ts`. After write, no further normalisation
runs.

### AC-B3 — Hydration is a pure read

`settings.ts:232` and `agent-config-normalization.ts:108`
(`hydrateRegisteredAgentConfig`) become pass-through reads. Either
removed or reduced to type validation only.

### AC-B4 — Migration handled

If existing `settings.toml` files contain non-canonical agent
entries, they are auto-migrated on first read. The migration is
idempotent and tested.

### AC-B5 — Self-audit grep #2 returns zero hits

After this and the P0 sibling ship:

```bash
grep -rn "toActiveAgentInfo\|normalizeAgentIdentity" src/ \
  | grep -v "src/lib/agent-identity.ts" \
  | grep -v "src/app/beats/" \
  | grep -v "__tests__"
```

Returns zero hits.

## Both paths

### AC-COMMON — Quality gates green

- `bun run lint`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run build`

### AC-COMMON — Umbrella foolery-2e97 AC-6 passes

After this knot AND the P0 sibling ship, the umbrella's
self-audit greps all return zero unsanctioned hits.
